summary: A.L.E.X. v2.1 backend REGRESSION after requirements.txt simplification (removed google-api-core/grpcio-status transitive pins). All 25/25 pytest tests pass in 41.3s. Verified: root/version, categories (defaults + CRUD), debts CRUD, settings PUT, monthly-entries dict-schema upsert, dashboard structure+math, rebalance endpoint (structure, skip-overweight, sum-to-budget, auto-detect, no-targets), allocation_targets on dashboard, backup export/import, migration integrity, and AI /ai/analyze (confirms emergentintegrations import still works with simplified requirements.txt).
passed: GET /api/categories returns 7 default categories with correct kinds
GET /api/dashboard returns categories, debts, allocation_targets, allocation_target_sum, fsi, fondo_emergenza, history
GET /api/rebalance?monthly_budget=500&months=12 returns valid suggestions, overweight cats get 0, sum equals budget
POST /api/monthly-entries with dict schema (contributions/balances/debt_payments) upserts by mese preserving id
PUT /api/settings persists new-schema keys and strips legacy
GET /api/backup/export returns all 6 collections with version 2.0
POST /api/ai/analyze returns valid JSON with decisione_finale in {BUY,HOLD,SELL,WATCH} - emergentintegrations OK after req.txt cleanup
critical_code_review_comments: requirements.txt simplification (removed pinned google-api-core/grpcio-status) had NO functional regression on any tested endpoint. Safe for Render deploy.
server.py still at ~836 lines - splitting into route modules remains a nice-to-have (carried over from iteration_3, not a regression).
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_* UUID-prefixed categories/debts/entries; all cleaned up in finally blocks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Requirements.txt simplification verified. If Render deploy still fails, issue is environment/pip resolver related, not backend code. All 25 pytest tests remain a solid regression base.
